name: Code Implementation
trigger: code, implement, build, fix bug, refactor

steps.label: Task involves code?
steps.description: Determine if the task requires code changes

steps.label: Trivial one-liner?
steps.description: Single sed, one config value, rename

steps.label: Do it directly
steps.description: Execute the trivial change yourself

steps.label: Write TASK.md
steps.description: Write TASK.md with: what to build/fix, acceptance criteria, files involved, constraints

steps.label: Spawn Codex
steps.description: Use coding-agent skill to delegate to Codex CLI

steps.label: Review output
steps.description: Review Codex output when complete

steps.label: Verify
steps.description: Test before reporting (Verification Protocol). Never claim fixed without evidence.

description: Delegate ALL code implementation to OpenAI Codex CLI. The orchestrator's role is awareness, strategy, and decision-making — not writing code. The ONLY exception is trivial one-liners where spawning an agent costs more than doing it. Before delegating: understand the system, read source code, trace data flow, identify root cause with evidence, specify the exact fix in TASK.md with line numbers and data samples, define testable acceptance criteria.
enforcementLayer: Shield: Direct Coding Gate (blocks code writes, forces Codex)

name: Delegation Protocol

steps.label: Read source code
steps.description: Understand the system — read source code, trace data flow

steps.label: Identify root cause
steps.description: Find the root cause with evidence, not guesswork

steps.label: Specify exact fix
steps.description: Write TASK.md with line numbers, data samples, and exact changes

steps.label: Define acceptance criteria
steps.description: Testable criteria: what command proves it works?

steps.label: Delegate to Codex
steps.description: Spawn Codex with TASK.md in target workdir

steps.label: Monitor progress
steps.description: Check process logs, handle questions

steps.label: Verify result
steps.description: Run acceptance criteria. Never claim fixed without deterministic proof.

description: Before delegating to Codex: (1) understand the system yourself — read source code, trace data flow, (2) identify the root cause with evidence, (3) specify the exact fix in TASK.md with line numbers and data samples, (4) define testable acceptance criteria. 'Investigate and fix' is never an acceptable task specification. The orchestrator does the thinking; Codex does the typing.
enforcementLayer: Shield: Delegation Gate (validates TASK.md before Codex)

name: Research Protocol

steps.label: Evaluate query depth
steps.description: Is this a simple factual lookup or deep research?

steps.label: web_search (Brave)
steps.description: Quick lookup via Brave search for simple facts

steps.label: Spawn researcher agent
steps.description: Delegate to researcher agent with Perplexity Pro

steps.label: Wait for completion
steps.description: Push-based — agent auto-announces when done

steps.label: Read output
steps.description: Read from research/outputs/<filename>.md

steps.label: Synthesize & present
steps.description: Summarize findings and present to Manolo

description: Two tiers of research. Quick lookup: use web_search (Brave API) for simple factual questions. Deep research: ALWAYS spawn the 'researcher' agent (Perplexity Pro) for thorough investigation. Never use web_search + training data for deep research. If Manolo says 'research this' → spawn researcher. No exceptions.
enforcementLayer: Shield: Research Gate Layer 6e (forces Perplexity for deep research)

name: Config Change Protocol

steps.label: Read current config
steps.description: gateway config.get to see current state

steps.label: Plan the change
steps.description: Identify exact JSON path and value

steps.label: Apply via config.patch
steps.description: Use gateway config.patch — NEVER edit directly

steps.label: Patch rejected?
steps.description: Did config.patch accept or reject the change?

steps.label: STOP & investigate
steps.description: Read the error. Ask Manolo if unclear. Do NOT bypass.

steps.label: Gateway auto-restarts
steps.description: Gateway restarts automatically on accepted config change

steps.label: Verify change works
steps.description: Test: trigger heartbeat, check config.get, etc.

description: Never edit openclaw.json directly. Always use 'gateway config.patch' for configuration changes. This ensures proper validation, hot-reloading, and audit trail. Direct file edits bypass safety checks and can corrupt the gateway state.
enforcementLayer: Shield: Config Change Gate Layer 6f (requires backup before config writes) + Protected Path Gate

name: Model Selection Protocol

steps.label: Can it be done without AI?
steps.description: Script, cron, deterministic — no model needed

steps.label: Use deterministic solution
steps.description: No AI needed. Zero token cost.

steps.label: Is a free model available?
steps.description: Check: gpt-5.3-codex (OpenAI Pro), gemini-flash (free tier), ollama (local)

steps.label: Use free model
steps.description: gpt-5.3-codex, gemini-flash, or ollama depending on task

steps.label: Cost-aware selection
steps.description: Match task complexity to model tier

steps.label: Heartbeat/routine → Gemini Flash
steps.description: Free, fast, good enough for checks

steps.label: Background work → Codex
steps.description: Free via Pro subscription

steps.label: Complex reasoning → Opus
steps.description: Main session only, subscription flat rate

description: Hierarchy: deterministic first (scripts, cron, launchd — zero token cost), then free models, then cost-aware selection. Prefer free > cheap > expensive. Use Opus for complex reasoning, Haiku for routine tasks, local models for repetitive work. Every AI call should justify why it can't be solved deterministically.
enforcementLayer: Shield: Model Selection Gate Layer 6g (blocks expensive models for routine tasks) + Model Cost Gate Layer 10d

name: Autonomous Development
trigger: new system, new architecture, strategic decision

steps.label: Architect
steps.description: Design solution from requirements

steps.label: Self-debate (≥5 rounds)
steps.description: Stress-test adversarially with Alpha/Beta personas. MANDATORY.

steps.label: Opportunity scan
steps.description: What else does this enable? Content, tools, benchmarks?

steps.label: Human perspective
steps.description: Apply known Manolo challenge patterns from memory

steps.label: Deterministic audit
steps.description: What can be code instead of AI? Map each component. MANDATORY.

steps.label: Write TASK.md
steps.description: Specify implementation for Codex

steps.label: Build (delegate to Codex)
steps.description: Spawn Codex with implementation task

steps.label: Verify
steps.description: Test with deterministic commands. ALWAYS after build.

description: Mandatory for design-level work. Seven composable operations: Architect (system design), Self-debate (>=5 adversarial rounds), Opportunity scan (market/tech landscape), Human perspective (empathy check), Deterministic audit (can this be non-AI?), Build (implementation), Verify (testing). Minimum before any build: self-debate + deterministic audit. Do NOT skip to build because the design 'feels ready.'
enforcementLayer: Shield: Autonomous Development Gate Layer 6h (requires self-debate before design-level work)
